summarization.a: The line chart shows film thickness as a function of SF₆ plasma pulses during half-cycle A. The thickness remains constant, indicating no net material removal during this half-cycle alone.
summarization.b: The line chart shows film thickness as a function of TMA pulses during half-cycle B. The film thickness does not change, indicating that this half-cycle alone does not produce etching.
summarization.c: The line chart shows film thickness as a function of full ALE cycles consisting of SF₆ plasma followed by TMA exposure. The thickness decreases linearly with cycle number at a rate of approximately 3.1 Å per cycle, demonstrating controlled atomic layer etching.

c.question: What is the etch rate during the full ALE cycle?

a.question: Which process conditions lead to controlled film thinning?
a.answer: Alternating SF₆ plasma exposure, TMA dosing, ALE cycles combining  half-steps

a.question: How does film thickness change across the half-cycle and full-cycle processes?
a.answer: During the individual half-cycles (SF₆ plasma or TMA exposure alone), the film thickness remains constant, indicating no net etching. In contrast, when the two steps are combined into a full ALE cycle, the film thickness decreases linearly with cycle number

b.question: Does either half-cycle alone produce measurable film thinning?
